sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: The NCCN Guidelines for Small Bowel Adenocarcinoma (SBA) emphasize a multimodal, multidisciplinary approach due to the rarity and complexity of this disease. Participation in clinical trials is strongly encouraged for all stages, as high-level evidence is limited. Treatment recommendations are often extrapolated from colorectal cancer (CRC) data. All recommendations are Category 2A (uniform consensus based on lower-level evidence) unless otherwise noted [SBA-1, SBA-2].

sections.heading: MDT Requirements
sections.content: The NCCN Panel strongly recommends multidisciplinary team evaluation for all patients with SBA. This is particularly critical for: 1) duodenal tumors when neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemoradiotherapy is being considered, 2) cases of locally advanced or unresectable disease to assess resectability and conversion potential, 3) selection for complex surgical procedures like pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple), and 4) management of peritoneal metastases where cytoreductive surgery/HIPEC may be considered (though HIPEC is not recommended outside of clinical trials) [SBA-E, MS-9, MS-10].

sections.heading: Performance Status Guide
sections.content: A patient's performance status (PS) is a critical determinant for treatment selection, especially regarding intensive versus non-intensive systemic therapy for advanced disease. While the guidelines do not specify a universal PS cutoff, they state: For initial therapy of metastatic SBA where intensive therapy is not recommended (e.g., older patients), a geriatric assessment should be completed to aid in predicting treatment risks. Non-intensive options like fluorouracil/leucovorin or capecitabine ± bevacizumab are preferred over more toxic regimens like FOLFOX, CAPEOX, or FOLFIRINOX [SBA-D 1 of 12, footnote h].

nodes.label: Surgical Resection with En Bloc Lymphadenectomy
Non-Intensive Regimen (e.g., FU/LV or Capecitabine ± Bev)

edges.label: High-Risk Features (Stage II high-risk, Stage III)

cards.title: Curative Intent
cards.detail: Primary treatment is surgical resection with en bloc removal of regional lymph nodes. Adjuvant chemotherapy is considered based on pathologic stage and risk factors. For locally advanced or unresectable disease, conversion to resectable status via neoadjuvant therapy is a goal. A multidisciplinary team (MDT) evaluation is crucial for treatment planning, especially for duodenal tumors where chemoradiotherapy may be considered [SBA-2, SBA-4, SBA-6, SBA-7].

cards.title: Palliative Intent
cards.detail: Treatment goals are prolonging survival, controlling symptoms, and maintaining quality of life. Options include systemic therapy (chemotherapy, immunotherapy), palliative procedures (diversion, stenting) for obstruction, and clinical trials. Multidisciplinary evaluation is recommended to assess for potential conversion to resectable disease or for palliative interventions [SBA-3, SBA-5, SBA-8].
